message:
fix(config): wire path_topology through TOML parser + config CLI (#10)

Closes #10. The env var path was advertised but partially wired:

- `RCH_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT` and `RCH_ALIAS_PROJECT_ROOT` *did* take
  effect at the runtime path-normalization layer (env-override step
  applied directly to RchConfig.path_topology before to_policy()).
- But `[path_topology]` in user/project TOML files silently dropped:
  PartialRchConfig had no path_topology field, so serde::Deserialize
  swallowed the section, apply_layer had nothing to merge, and the
  config-tracked load returned defaults.
- And the config CLI surface (`rch config get`, `rch config show`,
  `rch config validate`) didn't list path_topology keys at all, so
  users got "unknown configuration key" even when typing the
  exact key the binary documented.

Net effect: users couldn't verify the env var was applied, couldn't
override roots from TOML, and reasonably concluded the env var was
broken too (the workaround in the bug report — symlinking
/data/projects to their actual root — was the only way to make it
work).

Fix:

- rch/src/config.rs:
  * New `PartialPathTopologyConfig` mirroring the `Option<String>`
    shape, added to PartialRchConfig with `#[serde(default)]`.
  * apply_layer now merges path_topology fields from the partial
    layer, treating empty strings as unset to match
    PathTopologyConfig::to_policy semantics (empty TOML value
    shouldn't shadow the env var).
- rch/src/commands/config.rs:
  * collect_value_sources now pushes `path_topology.canonical_root`
    and `path_topology.alias_root` with their effective values
    (configured or compiled-in default), so `rch config get` and
    `rch config validate` recognize them.
  * config_show now prints a `[path_topology]` section with both
    roots and their source attribution, so users can see at a glance
    whether their override is in effect and where it came from.

Verification:
- `rch config get path_topology.canonical_root` now returns the
  effective root (default or env-overridden).
- `rch config show` prints a `[path_topology]` block with source
  attribution.
- Smoke-tested with `env RCH_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT=/home/ubuntu` —
  config get returns `/home/ubuntu`, config show shows it as
  `from environment variable RCH_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT`, and the
  hook layer's debug log shows `verified_within_root=...root=/home/ubuntu`
  on a project under the new root.
- New regression tests:
  * test_path_topology_section_loaded_from_toml — pins the contract
    that `[path_topology]` in user TOML lands in
    config.path_topology with UserConfig source.
  * test_path_topology_env_overrides_toml — pins the env-over-TOML
    precedence so future config-load refactors can't regress this
    bug class.
- `cargo test --workspace`: clean, zero failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -481,6 +481,38 @@ pub fn config_show(show_sources: bool, ctx: &OutputContext) -> Result<()> {
         )
     );
 
+    // Path topology (issue #10): always show the effective root paths
+    // so users can verify that env-var or TOML overrides were picked up.
+    let canonical_root = config
+        .path_topology
+        .canonical_root
+        .clone()
+        .unwrap_or_else(|| {
+            rch_common::path_topology::DEFAULT_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT.to_string()
+        });
+    let alias_root = config.path_topology.alias_root.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {
+        rch_common::path_topology::DEFAULT_ALIAS_PROJECT_ROOT.to_string()
+    });
+    println!("\n{}", style.highlight("[path_topology]"));
+    println!(
+        "  {} = {}",
+        style.key("canonical_root"),
+        format_with_source(
+            "path_topology.canonical_root",
+            &style.value(&format!("\"{canonical_root}\"")),
+            &value_sources
+        )
+    );
+    println!(
+        "  {} = {}",
+        style.key("alias_root"),
+        format_with_source(
+            "path_topology.alias_root",
+            &style.value(&format!("\"{alias_root}\"")),
+            &value_sources
+        )
+    );
+
     // Show config file locations
     println!(
         "\n{}",
@@ -696,6 +728,37 @@ pub(super) fn collect_value_sources(
         sources,
     );
 
+    // Path topology overrides (issue #10). The runtime path-normalization
+    // layer already supported these via env var, but the config CLI surface
+    // didn't list them, so `rch config get path_topology.canonical_root`
+    // failed with "unknown configuration key" and `rch config show`
+    // hid them entirely. Surface the effective value (configured or
+    // compiled-in default) so users can verify their overrides took.
+    let canonical_root_effective = config
+        .path_topology
+        .canonical_root
+        .clone()
+        .unwrap_or_else(|| {
+            rch_common::path_topology::DEFAULT_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT.to_string()
+        });
+    push_value_source(
+        &mut values,
+        "path_topology.canonical_root",
+        canonical_root_effective,
+        sources,
+    );
+    let alias_root_effective = config
+        .path_topology
+        .alias_root
+        .clone()
+        .unwrap_or_else(|| rch_common::path_topology::DEFAULT_ALIAS_PROJECT_ROOT.to_string());
+    push_value_source(
+        &mut values,
+        "path_topology.alias_root",
+        alias_root_effective,
+        sources,
+    );
+
     values
 }
